carousel.title: Discover our games
carousel.subtitle: From heart-pounding strategy battles to GPS-driven quests, find the perfect game for your next event.

home.kicker: The #1 platform for real-world team games
home.headline: Make every event an unforgettable adventure
home.lede: JotiGames offers more than 13 unique game modes, real-time coordination and effortless setup for your team outings, (scouting) activities and (youth) camps. Create lasting memories.
home.ctaRegister: Start for free: create your first game
home.tagShortLong: Activities from 15 minutes to full days
home.panelLede: Do you have your codes? Jump straight into a live game - no download required.
home.panelFooter: Don't have any codes yet? Ask your game organizer for the team login details.
home.featuresTitle: Everything you need to play great games
home.featuresSubtitle: One platform, no hassle. From setup to scoreboard.
home.featureOneTitle: One dashboard to manage them all
home.featureOneText: Create games, manage teams, configure challenges, and monitor everything from one intuitive admin panel. No technical skills required.
home.featureTwoText: Live location tracking, instant score updates, team messages and dynamic game states keep every player engaged from start to finish.
home.featureThreeText: Whether it's a forest path, a city center or a campsite: our GPS games work everywhere. Ideal for youth groups, corporate events, and scouts.
home.stepsSubtitle: No complicated installation. No app downloads. Just make a game and play it.
home.step1Text: Choose from over 13 unique games — from card battles to GPS quests. Configure (map) points, timing and rules to suit your event.
home.step2Text: Share simple team codes with your players. Teams can join directly from any browser – no accounts or downloads required.
home.step3Text: Track the progress of each team in real time. Send messages, customize challenges and celebrate winners from your live dashboard.
home.ctaBottomTitle: Ready to create your next adventure?
home.ctaBottomText: Join us and use JotiGames to create unforgettable experiences.

about.title: The story behind JotiGames
about.subtitle: Born from scouting, built for every team that loves real adventure games.
about.missionText: We believe the best games take place outdoors, where teams collaborate, explore and challenge each other personally. JotiGames removes the technical barriers so organizers can focus on what's important: creating magical experiences for their groups.
about.offerText: 13+ out-of-the-box game modes, real-time team dashboards, GPS challenges and a powerful admin panel - all accessible from any browser. Whether you need a 15-minute energizer or an all-day championship, we've got you covered.
about.everyoneText: From individual participants to hundreds of teams: JotiGames scales effortlessly. Our platform handles the complexity so you can focus on the fun.
about.ctaText: Create a free account and start your first game in minutes.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about running and playing JotiGames.
faq.a1: JotiGames is a free platform for organizing real team-based games. Think GPS quests, strategy map battles, turf wars and more – all managed through one simple dashboard.
faq.q2: Do players need to create an account?
faq.a2: No! Players participate with a simple game code + team code that the organizer shares. No downloads, no sign-ups: just open the link and start playing.
faq.q3: Can I use JotiGames for outdoor activities?
faq.a3: Absolute! Most of our 13+ game modes are designed for outdoor play with GPS integration. They work great in forests, parks, cities, campsites – anywhere with a mobile signal.
faq.q4: How do I create and manage a game?
faq.a4: Create a free organizer account, choose a game type, configure your settings (map points, timing, rules), add teams and you're live! The whole process only takes a few minutes.
faq.q5: How many teams can play at the same time?
faq.a5: As much as you need! Each game supports multiple teams with unique login codes. We've handled events where dozens of teams were active at the same time.
faq.q6: Can I follow team progress during the game?
faq.a6: Yes! Your live dashboard shows real-time positions, scores, completed tasks and team states. You can also send messages and adjust game parameters on the fly.
faq.q7: How does my team participate in a game?
faq.a7: Your game organizer will give you a game code and team code. Go to the Team Login page, enter both codes and you'll be dropped straight into the action.
faq.q8: Does it work on phones and tablets?
faq.a8: Yes! JotiGames is completely browser-based and responsive - it works beautifully on phones, tablets and desktops. No app download required.
faq.q9: What happens if we lose the internet connection?
faq.a9: Don't worry, just reconnect using the same login details. Your progress is saved to the server so you can pick up where you left off.
faq.ctaText: Jump in and try it yourself – or contact us for help getting started.

monetisation.title: Choose a package that makes your event pop
monetisation.subtitle: From free starts to large-scale multi-day events: JotiGames grows with you. Clear minutes, predictable costs and maximum impact for your teams.
monetisation.plansSubtitle: These are the active packages that you can use immediately today.
monetisation.calculatorSubtitle: Enter your teams and playing time. We deliberately recommend a large bundle so that you do not run out of minutes in the middle of your event.
monetisation.calculatorUpsellHint: Advice includes a safety margin and room for growth so you can scale with confidence.
monetisation.planPitch: Perfect for teams that want maximum gaming experience with minimal organizational stress with {name}.
monetisation.noPlansFallback: There are currently no active packages set up. Please try again later.
monetisation.disabledFallback: Subscriptions are currently disabled on this platform.
monetisation.howItWorksBody: Each active game consumes 1 minute per team per minute. This way you pay exactly for real playing time, not for vague user limits. You retain control, even at larger events.
monetisation.formula: Consumption = minutes of playing time × number of teams × concurrent games
monetisation.factOne: Only active games count: stationary or completed games do not consume anything.
monetisation.factTwo: Top-ups are only used when your monthly bundle has run out.
monetisation.factThree: Unlimited package? Then you no longer have to pay attention to minutes.
monetisation.exampleOneHeadline: {minutes} minutes = approximately one 2-hour event with 5 teams
monetisation.exampleOneExtra: Ideal for a Saturday afternoon activity with one central game.
monetisation.exampleTwoHeadline: {minutes} minutes = multiple rounds with parallel teams
monetisation.exampleTwoMath: Calculation: {hours} hours × 60 × {teams} teams × {games} concurrent games = {total} minutes
monetisation.exampleTwoExtra: Strong for regional events where you plan several play moments throughout the day.
monetisation.exampleThreeHeadline: {minutes} minutes = serious scale for intensive programs
monetisation.exampleThreeMath: Calculation: {hours} hours × 60 × {teams} teams × {games} concurrent games = {total} minutes
monetisation.exampleThreeExtra: Perfect if you want many teams to play at the same time with room for extra rounds.
monetisation.bottomTitle: Ready to have every team on the edge of their seats?
monetisation.bottomText: Start small, scale quickly, and deliver a gaming experience that players will talk about for weeks to come.

birdsOfPrey.mapHint: Your location, your own eggs and visible enemy eggs are on the map.
birdsOfPrey.autoDroppedMessage: An egg has automatically been placed for your team.

birds_of_prey.load_failed: Birds of Prey configuration loading failed
birds_of_prey.save_failed: Birds of Prey configuration save failed

info.heroText: Birds of Prey turns your terrain into a living nest war. Teams move through the real world, leaving eggs at their current location, hunting nearby enemy eggs, and defending their own positions before rivals can strike.
info.panelPointOne: Every move counts, because visibility depends on real distance.
info.panelPointTwo: Protection zones reward teamwork rather than just speed.
info.panelPointThree: The live rankings keep the pressure up until the last minute.
info.sectionBattleTitle: A territory game where stealth and timing are more important than brute force
info.sectionBattleText: Birds of Prey feels constantly in motion, as the map never stands still. Eggs appear where teams actually walk, enemy objectives only become visible when you get close enough, and protection zones constantly change which risks are worth taking.
info.featureNestsText: Eggs remain active until an enemy destroys them. So every drop creates a target worth defending or tracking down again later.
info.featureVisibilityText: Enemy eggs only become visible when your team comes within the set radius. That rewards exploration and smart route choices.
info.featureProtectionText: An owner close enough to his egg blocks destruction. This allows you to attract raids, hold territory or pull rivals out of position.
info.sectionHowTitle: Quick to explain, strategic as soon as the hunt begins
info.sectionHowText: The team dashboard shows your own eggs, nearby enemy eggs and the live leaderboard. From there, the round turns into a constant cycle of exploration, placement, defense, and looting.
info.flowScoutText: Teams keep moving with live GPS positions. The closer you get to enemy nests, the more likely hidden eggs will appear on your map.
info.flowDropText: Drop eggs from your current location in the real world. Organizers can also enable automatic drops to keep the pressure high during the round.
info.flowRaidText: Visible enemy eggs can be destroyed once the owner is no longer close enough to protect them. Good timing turns a patrol into an ambush.
info.flowLeadText: Each successful destruction instantly updates the live score and leaderboard, giving every chase, diversion and counter-move visible weight.
info.playStylePatrol: Patrol your own cluster of eggs and avoid easy steals.
info.playStyleRaid: Send quick scouts deep into enemy territory to uncover vulnerable nests.
info.playStyleDecoy: Use visible movement as a distraction while a teammate comes from another direction.
info.playStyleRecover: Recover quickly from setbacks by dropping new eggs along the way instead of camping in one zone.
info.organizerTitle: Strong live control without losing the offside feeling
info.organizerText: Birds of Prey is easy to run during a real event. Organizers set radii and pace in advance, then monitor live egg activity and score changes from the admin view while teams stay fully engaged outdoors.
info.organizerConfigText: Adjust visibility radius, protection radius and auto-drop interval to switch between a compact sprint and a long strategic turf battle.
info.organizerLiveText: Admins get a live overview of teams, egg counts and score movements so they can closely monitor pressure and pace of play during the round.
info.organizerAccessibleTitle: Easy to understand, hard to master
info.organizerAccessibleText: The basic rules are simple enough for a quick briefing, while the range and protection mechanics give experienced teams real depth.
info.perfectForTitle: Use it when you want movement, pressure and memorable team stories
info.perfectForText: Birds of Prey works especially well in areas that invite patrols, shortcuts and sudden counter-attacks. It produces exactly the kind of stories that teams will retell for a long time.
info.perfectForWideFields: Large camp sites, forests and city games where movement really makes a difference.
info.perfectForMixedAges: Mixed ages who appreciate simple controls with tactical depth.
info.perfectForEvening: Evening programs where stealth, surprise and regrouping provide extra excitement.
info.perfectForCompetitive: Competitive events that require live standings and constant action.
info.ctaTitle: Ready to start your own nest war?
info.ctaText: Create a game, tailor the hunt to your terrain and send teams into a round full of bluffs, raids and ranking changes in the last minute.

admin.point_resource_required: Enable at least one resource for this point

team.map_hint: All trading points are visible. Points within range are highlighted.

info.heroText: Market Crash turns your playing field into a fickle market. Teams walk between trading points, building inventory, calculating risk and trying to maximize profits as prices continue to fluctuate live.
info.panelPointOne: Trading is not just about locations, but also about perfect timing.
info.panelPointTwo: Cash, inventory and score are directly related in the team interface.
info.panelPointThree: Price fluctuations mean that sitting still is almost always a risk.
info.battleTitle: An economic game in which the market is just as dangerous as your opponents
info.battleText: Market Crash feels special because movement, timing and calculations count at the same time. Teams must decide whether to buy now, wait longer, sell immediately or hold on to stock for a bigger hit.
info.featureOneText: Trading points have their own buy and sell prices, tick speed and fluctuation per resource.
info.featureTwoText: Cash, starting capital, stock and score are intertwined and make every decision tangible.
info.featureThreeText: Each trading point can offer different resources, prices and dynamics, making routes and specialization pay off.
info.howTitle: Accessible to start, deep enough for teams to actually act
info.howText: Teams see nearby trading points, their inventory and available cash. This makes trading easy to execute, but the real profit lies in reading market movement and choosing the right moment.
info.flowOneText: The organizer defines resources and per point how buying, selling and fluctuating may take place.
info.flowTwoText: Teams bring a point into reach and see what buying or selling opportunities are available there.
info.flowThreeText: Every trade immediately changes what a team can do later or can no longer afford.
info.flowFourText: Live price deltas and score movement make it clear who is not just running fast, but really trading smart.
info.playStyleOne: Don't just buy cheap; Also think about where you can realistically sell.
info.playStyleTwo: Keep some cash on hand so that an unexpected opportunity does not become prohibitively expensive.
info.playStyleThree: Have one team member read the market while others monitor route and timing.
info.playStyleFour: Dare to take profits on time; waiting for the perfect price is also a risk.
info.organizerTitle: Excellent for events where economy and movement can come together
info.organizerText: Market Crash is a strong mode when you want teams to calculate, plan and respond to live changes. The worker-driven market movement naturally keeps the game alive.
info.perfectForTitle: Perfect when you want to make teams feel real trading decisions
info.perfectForText: This mode works best on fields where different market points are logically connected and teams are given room to develop their own trading style.
info.perfectForOne: Older scout groups or competitive participants who enjoy economy and risk.
info.perfectForTwo: City games or large areas with multiple logical trade routes.
info.perfectForThree: Events where live fluctuation can cause surprising comebacks.
info.perfectForFour: Programs that want to be more than just running from point to point.
info.ctaTitle: Ready to build a market that really makes teams sweat?
info.ctaText: Set resources, prices and fluctuations and let teams discover how thin the line is between trading genius and a painful crash.

gameInfo.ctaText: Create a free organizer account or browse more game modes to find your perfect match.
gameInfo.flow1Text: Your team enters {name} with a simple code: no downloads required. Review the mission objectives and prepare for the challenge ahead.
gameInfo.flow2Text: Interact with real-world locations, complete challenges and use strategic game mechanics. Every action counts towards your team's score.
gameInfo.flow3Text: Coordinate your team's strategy, complete objectives efficiently and climb the live leaderboards. Celebrate your victory!
gameInfo.option1Text: {name} adapts to your event: configure it for a quick 15-minute energizer or an all-day championship, with custom rules.
gameInfo.option2Text: Every technician is designed for collaboration. Shared objectives, live updates and team challenges keep everyone engaged.
gameInfo.option3Text: Monitor each team in real time, adjust difficulty on the fly, send in-game messages, and manage scores from your admin dashboard.
gameInfo.perfectForItem4: Team building events for mixed ages

gameInfoDetailed.organizerCardOneTitle: Adjust the game to your terrain
gameInfoDetailed.organizerCardOneText: Adjust locations, rules and pace to suit your route, group and available time.
gameInfoDetailed.organizerCardTwoText: Use the admin view to see progress, pressure and score movements as teams play in the field.
gameInfoDetailed.organizerCardThreeTitle: Quick to explain, strong in execution
gameInfoDetailed.organizerCardThreeText: Teams can start quickly, while the rules retain enough depth for a competitive and memorable round.

exploding_kittens.name: Exploding Kittens
exploding_kittens.shortDescription: A high-stakes digital card game where teams scan real-world cards, collect powerful combos and try to survive while sabotaging rivals. One wrong draw can mean the end of the game!
exploding_kittens.subtitle: The ultimate strategic card battle: Defuse, Deceive and Dominate.

geohunter.name: GeoHunter
geohunter.shortDescription: Navigate to GPS waypoints scattered across your playing field, solve location-based puzzles and race to complete your mission before other teams. Perfect for exploring new areas!
geohunter.subtitle: GPS-powered exploration meets puzzle solving in the ultimate outdoor quest.

blindhike.name: Blind Hike
blindhike.shortDescription: Your map is distorted, your compass may be lying and you have limited markings. Trust your team's instincts and communication to navigate to the hidden target location.
blindhike.subtitle: Trust, communication and sharp instincts: can you find the hidden target?

resource_run.name: Resource Run
resource_run.shortDescription: Race between resource points, collect strategic materials and decide when to bank or move on. Smart route planning and quick decisions separate winners from runners-up.
resource_run.subtitle: Collect, protect and outsmart the competition in this fast-paced resource race.

territory_control.name: Territory Control
territory_control.shortDescription: Claim zones on the map by physically visiting them and then defend your territory against rival teams. Balance attack and defense as the map lights up with contested areas.
territory_control.subtitle: Conquer the map, claim zones, defend territory and outsmart your rivals.

market_crash.name: Market Crash
market_crash.shortDescription: Visit trading points, buy low, sell high and time the market perfectly. But be careful: prices fluctuate dynamically and one bad trade can wipe out your profits. Fortune favors the bold!
market_crash.subtitle: Trade smart, time the market and build your fortune before the crash hits.

crazy_88.name: Crazy 88
crazy_88.shortDescription: Race to complete 88 wild, creative and hilarious challenges! Take photos, record videos and submit your best attempts for the game master to review. Energy and creativity win the day.
crazy_88.subtitle: 88 wild challenges, unlimited laughs, one epic competition.

courier_rush.name: Courier Rush
courier_rush.shortDescription: Accept delivery missions, plan efficient routes between pick-up and drop-off points and race against the clock. Coordinate with your team to maximize deliveries and achieve top scores.
courier_rush.subtitle: Plan routes, race against the clock and make your way to victory.

echo_hunt.name: Echo Hunt
echo_hunt.shortDescription: Hidden beacons are scattered across the playing field. Use radar-like clues to track them down, get to them before your rivals and claim them for your team. Observation is everything.
echo_hunt.subtitle: Follow hidden beacons, defeat your rivals and claim the ultimate prize.

checkpoint_heist.name: Checkpoint Heist
checkpoint_heist.shortDescription: High-value checkpoints appear on the map. Conquer them by visiting them in person while rival teams try to dispute your claims. Hold control points longer for bigger point multipliers!
checkpoint_heist.subtitle: Conquer, battle and hold high-quality checkpoints to dominate the leaderboard.

pandemic_response.name: Pandemic Response
pandemic_response.shortDescription: Hotspots spread across the map! Coordinate your team to respond to emergencies, gather supplies, and contain the spread. Every second counts in this cooperative challenge.
pandemic_response.subtitle: Respond to emergencies, coordinate supplies and save the day.

birds_of_prey.name: Birds of Prey
birds_of_prey.shortDescription: Drop eggs on the map to score points, protect your nests from raiders and destroy enemy eggs when you see them. An exciting mix of strategy, stealth and real-world movement.
birds_of_prey.subtitle: Trap, protect and plunder: the ultimate GPS territory game.

code_conspiracy.name: Code Conspiracy
code_conspiracy.shortDescription: Discover cryptic strings of code, solve multi-step puzzles and submit solutions before rival teams crack them. Combine logic, teamwork and speed to unlock the conspiracy.
code_conspiracy.subtitle: Crack codes, solve puzzles, outsmart the conspiracy.

blindhike.map_pick_hint: Click on the map to place the target marker.
blindhike.target_location_help: Set the end goal for teams to estimate and achieve.
blindhike.target_latitude_help: Latitude of the hidden destination in decimal degrees.
blindhike.target_longitude_help: Longitude of the hidden destination in decimal degrees.
blindhike.obstacles_help: Apply map distortions to make orientation more difficult for teams.
blindhike.horizontal_flip_help: Flip the map left-right for all teams.
blindhike.vertical_flip_help: Flip the map top-bottom for all teams.
blindhike.scale_factor_help: Scales perceived distance: 1.0 = normal, higher = further, lower = closer.
blindhike.rotation_help: Rotate the map view by degrees (0-360) for all teams.
blindhike.constraints_help: Limit marker use so teams have to place more strategically.
blindhike.max_markers_help: Maximum number of markers per team (empty = no limit).
blindhike.marker_cooldown_help: Number of seconds of waiting time between marker placements per team.
blindhike.finish_radius_meters_help: A team finishes when a marker is placed within this radius of the goal.
blindhike.finish_radius_invalid: Finish radius must be at least 1 meter
blindhike.load_failed: Failed to load Blind Hike configuration
blindhike.save_failed: Failed to save Blind Hike configuration

info.heroText: Blind Hike lets teams navigate with a map that can be mirrored, rotated or scaled. You don't win by running blind, but by finishing with as few markers as possible within the finish radius of the hidden goal.
info.panelPointOne: Map distortions break your intuition and make communication more important than speed.
info.panelPointTwo: Every marker helps, but too many markers will make your finish less efficient.
info.panelPointThree: Live finish status creates excitement as multiple teams get close to the goal.
info.battleTitle: A navigation game in which false certainty is your biggest enemy
info.battleText: Blind Hike works because the terrain remains real, but your map sense does not. Teams must test information, correct each other's assumptions and dare to choose when one marker is smart and when it costs too much.
info.featureOneText: Organizers can use horizontal or vertical flips, scale changes and rotation to make even familiar environments surprising.
info.featureTwoText: Markers are not only tools but also a measuring stick for efficiency. This makes every placement a strategic choice.
info.featureThreeText: A team is only finished when a marker falls within the set finish radius. So being close is not the same as being ready.
info.howTitle: Quick to understand, difficult to master once distortion starts
info.howText: Teams see their distorted map, place markers and, with limited certainty, try to find the hidden objective. The further the round progresses, the more important calm consultation and smart corrections become.
info.flowOneText: The organizer sets the target, finish radius and any flips, rotation or scale distortion.
info.flowTwoText: Teams quickly notice that their normal sense of direction is not quite right and have to recalibrate their perception.
info.flowThreeText: Markers help to test assumptions, but too many markers work against your end result.
info.flowFourText: The first marker within the finish radius counts, but teams that navigate more efficiently finish stronger.
info.playStyleOne: Work with one navigator and one controller so that incorrect assumptions are noticed more quickly.
info.playStyleTwo: Use early markers to spot pattern errors in the map, not to blindly lay a trail.
info.playStyleThree: Stay calm when two routes seem logical; Blind Hike rewards teams that test hypotheses.
info.playStyleFour: Save markers for the endgame, when precision becomes more important than speed.
info.organizerTitle: A lot of control for the management, a lot of uncertainty for the teams
info.organizerText: Blind Hike is ideal if you want a navigation game that requires little explanation but immediately provides excitement. By varying finish radius, marker restrictions and map distortions you can make the same terrain feel different every time.
info.perfectForTitle: Ideal if you want to make teams doubt their own sense of direction
info.perfectForText: Blind Hike excels in terrain that teams would normally navigate quickly. It is precisely there that the distorted map makes the difference tangible and memorable.
info.perfectForOne: Scouting activities in which communication and trust are more important than pure speed.
info.perfectForTwo: Evening or camp programs where confusion and perseverance create beautiful stories.
info.perfectForThree: Compact playing fields on which you still get a lot of tactical depth with map deformation.
info.perfectForFour: Groups that enjoy puzzle-like navigation instead of straight-forward walking.
info.ctaTitle: Ready to deliberately take teams off course?
info.ctaText: Set a goal, turn the map, limit the markers and let teams discover how much consultation is needed to really hit the mark.

info.kicker: GPS quiz tour in the field
info.heroText: GeoHunter combines terrain exploration with questions that only become relevant when a team actually reaches a location. This makes each point feel like a small voyage of discovery: first navigate, then answer intelligently.
info.panelPointOne: POIs only become interesting once your team physically reaches them.
info.panelPointTwo: Questions can be open or multiple choice and are validated server-side.
info.panelPointThree: Retry rules give the organizer full control over pressure and retakes.
info.battleTitle: An exploration game in which knowledge only counts when you are in the right place
info.battleText: GeoHunter feels strong because teams can't build their score from a standstill. First the team must move smartly, then provide the right answer. This keeps the game physically and mentally balanced.
info.featureOneText: Locations are located in the playing field and only come to life when a team is actually near them.
info.featureTwoText: Open questions and multiple choice questions provide variety in pace and difficulty.
info.featureThreeText: The organizer determines whether teams can try again and how long they have to wait.
info.howTitle: A simple combination of walking, discovering and smart answers
info.howText: The team dashboard shows POIs on the map and opens query interaction as soon as a location comes into range. From there it's all about reading calmly, answering correctly and dealing smartly with any retries.
info.flowOneText: The organizer places interesting points, chooses question formats and determines the score value per location.
info.flowTwoText: Teams navigate to a location and bring it physically within reach.
info.flowThreeText: The question is checked server-side, including multiple choice validation and open answer comparison.
info.flowFourText: When retries are enabled, extra tension arises around timing and retakes.
info.playStyleOne: Divide the team between navigation and question focus so that you make fewer mistakes under time pressure.
info.playStyleTwo: Don't use retries mindlessly; sometimes it is smarter to move on to the next POI.
info.playStyleThree: Read multiple choice questions carefully, because server-side validation does not allow for sloppiness.
info.playStyleFour: Plan POI order by range and points, not just what's closest.
info.organizerTitle: Perfect for terrain exploration with built-in knowledge layer
info.organizerText: GeoHunter is ideal when you want to let teams discover an environment and at the same time ask substantive or thematic questions. The game is easy to scale from light-hearted to seriously educational.
info.perfectForTitle: Use it for routes where discovery and learning must reinforce each other
info.perfectForText: GeoHunter feels at home in locations where interesting places, stories or themes can become part of the gaming experience.
info.perfectForOne: Scouting routes, city explorations and thematic camp activities.
info.perfectForTwo: Educational or substantive assignments that you want to physically anchor in an environment.
info.perfectForThree: Teams that like to move but also enjoy knowledge questions or puzzles.
info.perfectForFour: Events where retrievals and question formats must be able to differ per target group.
info.ctaTitle: Ready to turn your terrain into a smart exploration?
info.ctaText: Place your POIs, add questions and let teams experience how powerful a good mix of navigation and knowledge can be.

admin.lat_lon_both: Latitude and longitude must both be filled in for geo-locked mode
admin.review_empty: No entries assigned at this time

info.heroText: Crazy 88 is the game mode for teams that score with courage, creativity and perseverance. Assignments are completed in real life, submitted as proof and only converted into points after assessment.
info.panelPointOne: The real tension lies in carrying out assignments and having them approved.
info.panelPointTwo: Statuses such as pending, approved and rejected provide constant feedback.
info.panelPointThree: Categories and visibility make the game widely applicable for any event.
info.battleTitle: A chaotic, energetic game that remains tightly managed
info.battleText: Crazy 88 works because creativity and speed come together, but management always has the final say through assessment. This keeps it fun and fair, even when teams push the boundaries.
info.featureOneText: Teams earn points by completing assignments and submitting original evidence.
info.featureTwoText: Pending, approved and rejected make progress visible and keep teams focused on what still matters.
info.featureThreeText: Admins review entries live, so that game quality and safety are monitored.
info.howTitle: Always something to do, but never without consequence
info.howText: Teams choose an assignment, carry it out and submit evidence. Then the wait for assessment begins, which creates additional tension and forces teams to smartly spread their time across multiple assignments.
info.flowOneText: The organizer sets up tasks, points and any visibility or location restrictions.
info.flowTwoText: Teams select assignments and immediately go into practice to collect evidence.
info.flowThreeText: A submission goes into the management review stream and is given a visible status.
info.flowFourText: Only after acceptance do the points count and the team really move up in the rankings.
info.playStyleOne: Combine quick surefire assignments with a few high-risk entries.
info.playStyleTwo: Consider review time; not only executing but also smartly spreading yields profit.
info.playStyleThree: Make sure that evidence is immediately clear, so that good assignments do not fail due to doubt.
info.playStyleFour: Use categories to let the team play with energy, humor or precision.
info.organizerTitle: Perfect for management who wants to unleash creativity without losing control
info.organizerText: Crazy 88 is ideal for camps, evening programs and events where teams need to have visible fun. The review layer makes it safe, fair and easy to dose for different ages.
info.perfectForTitle: Use it when you want energy, humor and visible teamwork
info.perfectForText: This mode is at its best where teams can improvise, laugh and surprise each other with what they dare to submit.
info.perfectForOne: Evening programs, camp days and creative competition blocks.
info.perfectForTwo: Mixed groups in which not only speed but also inventiveness must count.
info.perfectForThree: Events where management likes to assess live and control the course of the game.
info.perfectForFour: Programs in which many small successes are more important than one big final mission.
info.ctaTitle: Ready to unleash a flood of creative assignments?
info.ctaText: Build your to-do list, decide how teams see them, and fill the leaderboard with daring, hilarious, or clever entries.

admin.config_subtitle: Configure settings, pick-up points and drop-off points
admin.max_pickups_range: Max active pickup locations must be between 1 and 25
admin.map_label: Pick-up points and drop-off points map

info.heroText: Courier Rush turns your playing field into a network of pick-up and drop-off points. Teams must confirm pickups, make the correct follow-up move and only win the real points at the drop-off.
info.panelPointOne: Pickups and drop-offs require pace and route insight.
info.panelPointTwo: The score is on the episode, not on the first action.
info.panelPointThree: Active locations on the map keep the game constantly moving.
info.battleTitle: A delivery game in which the second step determines the win
info.battleText: Courier Rush feels strong because teams don't just tap locations, they complete real delivery cycles. This creates choices about order, efficiency and the question of whether a detour is worthwhile or costs time.
info.featureOneText: Teams build up scores by completing missions. That gives the game more rhythm than individual checkpoints.
info.featureTwoText: Because several points are on the map at the same time, running line and pace immediately become part of the strategy.
info.featureThreeText: Collection and delivery modes are customizable, allowing you to make the game compact or dynamic and unpredictable.
info.howTitle: A clear game flow with enough room for smart route gains
info.howText: The team dashboard shows pickups, drop-offs and when a team comes into range to confirm an action. This makes the mission clear, but the difference is in how efficiently teams complete their rounds.
info.flowOneText: The organizer sets pickup and drop-off modes and places the relevant locations on the site.
info.flowTwoText: Teams reach a pick-up point and register the start of their delivery there.
info.flowThreeText: After the pickup, the choice of route becomes exciting: where can you deliver the fastest and smartest?
info.flowFourText: The drop-off confirmation earns server-side points and keeps the leaderboard moving.
info.playStyleOne: Plan not only the shortest route, but also the route with the least downtime between actions.
info.playStyleTwo: Don't waste time on pickups if the delivery line is inconvenient afterwards.
info.playStyleThree: Keep the team compact so that confirmations can happen in quick succession.
info.playStyleFour: Use the live leaderboard to decide when to play it safe and when to push.
info.organizerTitle: Strong for events where you want to combine movement and logistics
info.organizerText: Courier Rush works great when you want teams to do more than just tap locations. The two-step model immediately gives the game more flow and more tactical value per meter run.
info.perfectForTitle: Ideal for playing fields where smart routes are more important than pure sprint power
info.perfectForText: Courier Rush is at its best when teams are constantly switching between tasks and efficiency shows up in the scoring charts.
info.perfectForOne: Areas with multiple paths, zones or city blocks.
info.perfectForTwo: Groups that like movement, task logic and clear mission cycles.
info.perfectForThree: Events where you want score differences to arise due to route quality.
info.perfectForFour: Programs in which an active, modern logistics race fits well.
info.ctaTitle: Ready to turn your terrain into a delivery race?
info.ctaText: Place pickups and drop-offs, determine the rules of the network and show teams how quickly logistics can become exciting.

info.heroText: Resource Run is a pure card game in which teams claim resources from nodes to build up a score as quickly as possible. The simplicity is exactly the strength: move, reach, claim and move on.
info.panelPointOne: Nodes make the map immediately readable and action-oriented.
info.panelPointTwo: Claims deliver server-side points without noise or extra administration.
info.panelPointThree: The rankings remain constantly in motion due to the high pace of action.
info.battleTitle: A simple scoring concept that becomes surprisingly competitive
info.battleText: Because Resource Run has little friction between intention and action, every decision is immediately felt. That makes pacing, order and reading the terrain more important than complicated rules.
info.featureOneText: Each point on the map has a clear function: reach it, claim it and set the score in motion.
info.featureTwoText: The claim action appears within a radius and the team can move on to the next goal without detours.
info.featureThreeText: Due to the fast claim rhythm, teams constantly feel how their choices are reflected in the live mode.
info.howTitle: An accessible game that relies entirely on movement and rhythm
info.howText: The team dashboard shows resource nodes, their scope and leaderboard status. This means the game remains extremely accessible, while quick decisions and route selection still make the difference.
info.flowOneText: The organizer places resource points on the terrain with radius, color and score value.
info.flowTwoText: Teams choose their next target and bring the node into physical range.
info.flowThreeText: The promotion is confirmed and the points are credited server-side.
info.flowFourText: Those who continue to switch between nodes the fastest and smartest will build the most momentum.
info.playStyleOne: Don't waste time on doubtful points; in Resource Run the team with the best rhythm often wins.
info.playStyleTwo: Work with a clear route sequence so that the team does not have to choose again and again.
info.playStyleThree: Use the leaderboard to determine when to run safely and when to speed up.
info.playStyleFour: Keep the group compact, so that claim moments do not have to wait for each other.
info.organizerTitle: Perfect when you want an immediate pace of play without a complex briefing
info.organizerText: Resource Run is ideal for energetic moments at camp or event. You get immediate action on the map and a game that can be explained in a few minutes, but still remains competitive.
info.perfectForTitle: Ideal for fast cross-country rounds where simple doesn't have to be boring
info.perfectForText: This mode works best when you want a clear scoring model that still leaves enough room for smart route gains.
info.perfectForOne: Short energizers and active game blocks on camp grounds.
info.perfectForTwo: Groups that prefer to play straight away rather than hearing a lot of rules first.
info.perfectForThree: Tournaments where you want to see a lot of action and clear score differences.
info.perfectForFour: Areas with sufficient spread to make route choices relevant.
info.ctaTitle: Ready to put on a quick scoring expedition?
info.ctaText: Place your nodes, give them character with points and color and let teams experience how addictive a simple but razor-sharp claim loop can be.

admin.center_required: Mid latitude and longitude are required

info.heroText: Pandemic Response puts teams in the middle of an outbreak scenario where they must resolve hotspots and gather support pickups. This makes the game feel like a response operation: moving, prioritizing and acting under pressure.
info.panelPointOne: Hotspots and pickups require a clear division of tasks within the team.
info.panelPointTwo: Severity makes visible which incidents feel most urgent.
info.panelPointThree: Resolve and collect actions ensure a tight operational rhythm.
info.battleTitle: A field game that feels like a real response mission
info.battleText: Pandemic Response works because teams don't just chase points, they make choices based on urgency. Where are you going next, what are you leaving aside and what action will bring the team back into control fastest?
info.featureOneText: Incidents are visible with severity, making the map immediately feel like a changing crisis space.
info.featureTwoText: Pickups give the mode a more operational feel and keep teams from just jumping from hotspot to hotspot.
info.featureThreeText: Points for hotspot resolution come from the server config and keep the race fair and scalable.
info.howTitle: A clear response loop with room for strong priorities
info.howText: Teams see hotspots and pickups on the map and are given action options as soon as they come into range. The interface remains simple, while the prioritization of incidents provides real depth.
info.flowOneText: The organizer determines hotspot rules, severity behavior, pickups and any penalty pressure.
info.flowTwoText: Teams read the map, choose priority and bring the right location within reach.
info.flowThreeText: Pickups and hotspot resolves each have their own action flow and together form the game rhythm.
info.flowFourText: The live score and hotspot status immediately show which team is keeping the operation under best control.
info.playStyleOne: Don't blindly prioritize based on proximity; severity and point value often determine the real profit.
info.playStyleTwo: Make sure pickups support your route instead of slowing it down.
info.playStyleThree: Work with clear roles: navigator, decision maker and executor.
info.playStyleFour: Use live score information to decide when to play it safe and when to push it.
info.organizerTitle: Strong for theme events where collaboration and urgency are central
info.organizerText: Pandemic Response is ideal when you want a clear mission experience without complex explanations. Teams quickly understand the division of roles and the map immediately creates a strong crisis atmosphere.
info.perfectForTitle: Use it when you want to get teams to think like intervention teams
info.perfectForText: This mode is particularly strong for theme programs and scenario-based activities where movement and prioritization must come together.
info.perfectForOne: Scouting or training activities with a relief or mission theme.
info.perfectForTwo: Areas where multiple urgent goals may be visible at the same time.
info.perfectForThree: Groups that enjoy collaboration with clear roles and choices.
info.perfectForFour: Events where a live card should really feel like a crisis overview.
info.ctaTitle: Ready to send your teams into a response operation?
info.ctaText: Set up hotspots and pickups, determine the pressure of the scenario and let teams experience how exciting priorities become when everything demands attention at the same time.

info.heroText: Territory Control is about zones that not only have to be conquered but also defended. This creates a real area war: your score not only grows by arriving, but by cleverly determining which area is worth it.
info.panelPointOne: Zones give the map immediate strategic value and clear front lines.
info.panelPointTwo: Capture points reward action, while ownership increases longer-term pressure.
info.panelPointThree: Ownership and live score create visible shifts in power.
info.battleTitle: A card game in which terrain only really counts if you manage to hold onto it
info.battleText: Territory Control feels strong because a zone is more than a check mark on the map. Once teams start to see areas as owned, choices arise about defending, moving, forcing and distributing pressure.
info.featureOneText: Each zone is given radius, capture value and live ownership, making the map immediately strategically readable.
info.featureTwoText: Within range, teams claim a zone and control of the map immediately shifts.
info.featureThreeText: Because zone control has a direct effect on scoring pressure, changes in power are always noticeable.
info.howTitle: A simple claim action with many strategic consequences
info.howText: Teams see zones, their scope and ownership on the dashboard. This makes the operation simple, but the choice of which zone deserves attention provides the real depth.
info.flowOneText: The organizer places zones on the map and determines the capture value per area.
info.flowTwoText: Teams move into a relevant area and position themselves to claim.
info.flowThreeText: The capture action awards server-side points and establishes ownership.
info.flowFourText: After every claim follows the real question: hold on or immediately put pressure elsewhere?
info.playStyleOne: Don't see each zone as equally important; choose areas that support route and momentum.
info.playStyleTwo: Use capture moments as a signal to immediately reposition the team.
info.playStyleThree: Keep an eye on the leaderboard to know when it pays to hold on and when to push.
info.playStyleFour: Work with area priorities instead of always choosing the nearest zone.
info.organizerTitle: Strong for competitive groups that want to feel territory tangibly
info.organizerText: Territory Control is perfect when you want a game where the map constantly changes color and meaning. It is visually clear, easy to brief and strong for live overviews.
info.perfectForTitle: Use it when you want to put map control and rivalry front and center
info.perfectForText: This mode fits well on playing fields where different areas become logical points of contention and teams like to compete for space.
info.perfectForOne: Large camp sites or city areas with several clear zones.
info.perfectForTwo: Teams that like map printing, front lines and area thinking.
info.perfectForThree: Competitions where you want to show visually strong live overviews.
info.perfectForFour: Events in which conquering and preserving are both important.
info.ctaTitle: Ready to turn your map into a real territorial war?
info.ctaText: Place your zones, give them capture value and let teams feel how quickly control, pressure and pride can arise in an area.

info.heroText: Checkpoint Heist forces teams to capture checkpoints in the correct order. As a result, it's not just about getting somewhere, but about the pace at which your team unlocks the entire route and secures the big points.
info.panelPointOne: Teams cannot just score anywhere: the order determines the tension.
info.panelPointTwo: Active checkpoints keep the game clear and competitive at the same time.
info.panelPointThree: Each capture opens up the next step in the heist and instantly changes the race.
info.battleTitle: A linear route that still feels like a real race
info.battleText: Because each team must follow the same order, a clear competition is created without chaos. The difference is in pace, flawless movement and how quickly a team switches from capture to capture.
info.featureOneText: After each capture, the next checkpoint becomes relevant. That makes the route clear and keeps the race tight.
info.featureTwoText: Teams must physically arrive within the set radius. Smart walking and tight coordination continue to make the difference.
info.featureThreeText: Checkpoint values ​​come from the server config, so that the route can always be managed fairly and predictably.
info.howTitle: An accessible progression race with visible momentum
info.howText: On the team dashboard, each team can see which checkpoint is currently active and what progress has already been made. This keeps the focus simple: reach, conquer, unlock and stay in step with the fastest teams.
info.flowOneText: The organizer creates checkpoints, determines their order and sets radius and points.
info.flowTwoText: Teams move to the only checkpoint that matters to them now.
info.flowThreeText: Within range, the team can perform the capture and secure the points server-side.
info.flowFourText: After each capture, the next part of the heist automatically becomes relevant.
info.playStyleOne: Keep the pace high between captures; the real gain is often in transition time.
info.playStyleTwo: Use one team member to read the card and one to keep the rhythm going.
info.playStyleThree: Don't waste time taking detours to inactive locations.
info.playStyleFour: Treat each capture as a change point: move straight on to the next assignment.
info.organizerTitle: Strong for events where you want to show clear progress
info.organizerText: Checkpoint Heist is ideal when teams have to follow a clear but competitive route. The fixed order keeps the game easy to brief, while the live progression provides a lot of excitement.
info.perfectForTitle: Use it for tight route games with visible progress
info.perfectForText: Checkpoint Heist feels at home in areas where you want to combine a clear route with competition and pace.
info.perfectForOne: Jamborees and camps where multiple teams complete the same mission under time pressure.
info.perfectForTwo: City games or terrain races where route planning and physical execution come together.
info.perfectForThree: Activities where management wants to see live which teams are making the most progress.
info.perfectForFour: Groups that like clear goals and direct score feedback.
info.ctaTitle: Ready to convert your route into a real heist?
info.ctaText: Arrange checkpoints in order, determine the severity of each stop and let teams feel how quickly momentum can change a leaderboard.

info.heroText: Echo Hunt lets teams hunt for hidden beacons in the terrain. They only become relevant when you get close enough, making observation, movement and timing more important than blindly running to each point.
info.panelPointOne: Beacons really feel hidden until your crew brings them into range.
info.panelPointTwo: Claiming only happens when teams physically reach the right place.
info.panelPointThree: Live scoring pressure instantly turns every discovered beacon into a momentum moment.
info.battleTitle: A scavenger hunt that revolves around a sense of the right moment
info.battleText: Echo Hunt feels exciting because beacons only become valuable when teams actually bring them into proximity. As a result, not every path makes sense and a game of searching, recognizing and striking arises.
info.featureOneText: Beacons lie like targets in the field and only become relevant when a team gets close enough to claim them.
info.featureTwoText: Smart viewing and reading of the terrain prevents wasted meters and increases the chance of quick claims.
info.featureThreeText: Each claim moves the score live, so that teams continuously feel what their choices yield.
info.howTitle: Simple actions, but a lot of excitement in how you achieve them
info.howText: Teams move around the map, bring beacons into range and only then can they claim. This keeps the controls simple, while the hunt itself is full of tension and uncertainty.
info.flowOneText: The organizer sets up beacons with radius, points and visibility as desired for the site.
info.flowTwoText: Teams move around and actively look for places where a beacon comes into range.
info.flowThreeText: Only when the team is physically close enough can the claim be confirmed.
info.flowFourText: Each successful claim immediately changes the live score and forces other teams to respond.
info.playStyleOne: Don't walk randomly; work with a search pattern and a clear card reader.
info.playStyleTwo: Use the leaderboard to determine whether you should chase safe points or put more pressure.
info.playStyleThree: Keep teams compact so that a discovery leads to a real claim more quickly.
info.playStyleFour: Link observation to pace: those who play too cautiously miss opportunities.
info.organizerTitle: Strong when you want a pure search and claims experience
info.organizerText: Echo Hunt is ideal for events where movement and discovery should be central. It's easy to explain, but still provides a lot of excitement due to the combination of hidden goals and live score differences.
info.perfectForTitle: Perfect for fields where searching and recognizing is more fun than following fixed routes
info.perfectForText: Echo Hunt feels at its best in areas where teams really need to read where opportunities arise rather than simply running from point to point.
info.perfectForOne: Forests, camp areas and open fields with multiple logical search routes.
info.perfectForTwo: Groups that like to explore, observe and strike quickly.
info.perfectForThree: Scouting games where you want a clear action button, but an exciting search game around it.
info.perfectForFour: Competitions where live discoveries must be immediately noticeable in the rankings.
info.ctaTitle: Ready to send teams on a real signal hunt?
info.ctaText: Place your beacons, place the claim radii correctly and let teams experience how addictive it is to discover a hidden goal just in time.

info.heroText: Exploding Kittens on JotiGames combines physical card scans with digital team status, goal-oriented actions and live life pressure. This makes each card not only feel funny or mean, but directly decisive for how long your team will survive.
info.panelPointOne: Hand cards, pending actions and lives are all live in the team interface.
info.panelPointTwo: Actions such as attack, favor, nope and combos ensure constant interaction between teams.
info.panelPointThree: Scans in the field directly link physical moments of play to digital consequences.
info.battleTitle: A card game that is not just about luck, but about timing under pressure
info.battleText: Exploding Kittens works strongly in the field as physical maps and digital handling come together. Teams must not only pull and react, but also deal intelligently with pending attacks, defuses and interactive actions from rivals.
info.featureOneText: The team interface shows cards, lives and pending effects, so players always feel how precarious their position is.
info.featureTwoText: Attack, favor, nope and combo actions keep the interaction between teams lively and unpredictable.
info.featureThreeText: Real-world maps are linked to live game status and resolution via scan flows.
info.howTitle: Familiar card chaos, but with live team pressure on the screen
info.howText: Teams collect and play cards, respond to pending actions and try to preserve their lives. Because the system tracks actions and resolution live, it remains clear without removing the chaos.
info.flowOneText: The organizer puts together cards and can also prepare physical assets for the game via the card tools.
info.flowTwoText: Teams scan cards or use their hand to initiate actions into the game.
info.flowThreeText: Targeted actions and response cards such as nope or favor create pressure between teams.
info.flowFourText: Lives move live and ultimately determine who dominates or survives the round.
info.playStyleOne: Save your best response cards for times when pending pressure really does damage.
info.playStyleTwo: Don't just use favor and combo actions; timing determines more here than volume.
info.playStyleThree: Watch opponents' lives to see when an attack hurts the most.
info.playStyleFour: Keep team communication sharp between physical scans and digital resolution.
info.organizerTitle: Ideal if you want to combine physical game materials with live digital control
info.organizerText: Exploding Kittens is particularly suitable for events where teams like to play with real cards, but the management still wants to keep a live overview of status, actions and score or life pressure.
info.perfectForTitle: Use it when you want to collide humor, sabotage and suspense in one game
info.perfectForText: This mode fits perfectly with groups that enjoy direct interaction, unexpected twists and a playful form of rivalry.
info.perfectForOne: Indoor or tent programs with strong team interaction.
info.perfectForTwo: Groups that enjoy tactically thwarting each other.
info.perfectForThree: Events where physical game materials may be part of the experience.
info.perfectForFour: Competitions where laughter and excitement can land on the scoreboard at the same time.
info.ctaTitle: Ready to start a card battle with real live consequences?
info.ctaText: Prepare your deck, link scans to game actions and let teams discover how quickly a funny card can turn into pure panic.

info.heroText: Code Conspiracy is all about cracking rival team codes before they crack yours. The power lies in the mix of logical thinking, targeted pressure on opponents and the constant question of whether the next gamble is smart or too expensive.
info.panelPointOne: You consciously choose target teams; every attack is also a psychological move.
info.panelPointTwo: Correct points and penalties are determined completely server-side.
info.panelPointThree: Submission history makes progress and risk immediately visible.
info.battleTitle: A scoring duel in which information is often more valuable than speed
info.battleText: Code Conspiracy feels strong because every answer reveals something: about your reasoning, about an opponent and about how much risk your team can still take. This creates a smart duel instead of just blind entry.
info.featureOneText: Teams choose which other team they put pressure on. This makes focus changes and smart target choices important.
info.featureTwoText: Code validation, rewards and penalties come from the server config, so no one can cheat with submitted points.
info.featureThreeText: The team interface shows attempts and timing, making the match really feel like an ongoing operation.
info.howTitle: Few buttons, a lot of tension per input
info.howText: The team chooses a target team, enters a code and receives back whether the attempt had value. Because scores and penalties can be taken into account, every subsequent gamble remains a tactical choice.
info.flowOneText: The organizer determines code length, attempts, rewards, penalties and the game condition.
info.flowTwoText: Teams select a rival team and focus their attention on one opponent at a time.
info.flowThreeText: Each attempt is checked server-side against the target's real team code.
info.flowFourText: Correct guesses earn points, miscalculations can hurt and history determines the psychological game.
info.playStyleOne: Don't focus on all teams at once; a targeted attack often yields more than broad betting.
info.playStyleTwo: Keep track of which patterns aren't working so you don't waste attempts.
info.playStyleThree: When fines are on, discipline is more important than volume.
info.playStyleFour: Use timing: Sometimes waiting for better reasoning is stronger than one more quick guess.
info.organizerTitle: Excellent for groups that want to combine competition and thinking
info.organizerText: Code Conspiracy requires little terrain and no map, but still provides a lot of excitement. This makes it ideal for indoor locations, rain programs or moments when you want to bring rivalry and puzzle thinking together.
info.perfectForTitle: Perfect when you want to combine brain work, rivalry and scoring pressure
info.perfectForText: This game excels in settings where teams really want to outsmart each other, but where you want to keep logistics simple.
info.perfectForOne: Indoor programs or moments of rest with a competitive character.
info.perfectForTwo: Groups that like codes, logic and focused rivalry.
info.perfectForThree: Events where you want little physical infrastructure, but high tension.
info.perfectForFour: Tournaments in which score penalties and smart focus can make the difference.
info.ctaTitle: Ready to unleash a conspiracy between teams?
info.ctaText: Lay out code length, rewards and penalties well and show which team stays coolest when every input counts.

footer.tagline: The free platform for real team games. Built for scouts, youth groups and adventure enthusiasts around the world.

account.title: Account settings
account.profileDesc: Customize your display name and email address.
account.passwordDesc: Update your password. After making changes, you remain logged in.
account.paymentsDesc: Overview of all your payments and transactions

subscription.title: Subscription
subscription.freeForNow: Monetization is currently disabled. The platform is free to use!
subscription.noPlan: No active subscription. Choose a plan below.
subscription.cancellingAtPeriodEnd: Will be canceled at the end of the period
subscription.cancelConfirm: Are you sure you want to cancel your subscription? It remains active until the end of the current period.
subscription.cancelledFlash: Subscription will be canceled at the end of the period
subscription.stripeSecure: Payments are processed securely via Stripe
subscription.stripeNotConfigured: Payment provider not configured. Please contact the administrator.
